stories.title: Quote of the day by Aristotle: "The ideal man bears the accidents of life with dignity and…" - a powerful lesson on resilience, character and how people respond when life does not go according to plan

This approach focuses on how one handles setbacks, not on avoiding them.

stories.summary: German bond yields rose as oil prices increased amid U.S.-Iran tensions. This surge strengthened expectations for the European Central Bank to tighten monetary policy. Italy's 10-year bond yield also increased, widening the spread with German yields.

stories.title: Global Market: European shares dip as oil surge fuels inflation fears ahead of ECB meeting
stories.summary: European shares dipped slightly as oil prices climbed due to escalating US-Iran tensions. This surge revived inflation concerns and impacted investor sentiment ahead of key economic events. Energy stocks performed well, while travel and leisure shares faced pressure from higher fuel costs. Technology stocks saw gains as investors anticipated major US company earnings reports. Markets also await the European Central Bank's policy decision this week.

stories.title: India 10-year bond slumps to lowest in nearly a month as Middle East oil risks heat up

Foreign investor demand for Indian debt is expected to moderate following recent inflows.

stories.title: Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Sansera Engineering, 3 other auto ancillary stocks with upside of up to 28%. Here’s why

stories.title: Six tiny fossil jaws found in Australia have pushed the origin of its carnivorous marsupials back by 5 million years
stories.summary: A significant fossil discovery in Queensland has revealed Australia's oldest carnivorous marsupial. These ancient predators, dating back twenty-three million years, inhabited lush rainforests. The tiny fossil jaws belong to a newly identified species named Miyumba chrisdickmani. This finding fills a major gap in the continent's evolutionary history. It suggests dasyurids originated in wet habitats before adapting to drier landscapes.

This airborne contamination indicates that inhabitants may be exposed to lower concentrations of these toxins, which are believed to heighten the chances of ALS and various ailments. Unlike food-related toxins, mitigating exposure through breath poses a significant and complex challenge.

stories.title: Hunters carried trout past waterfalls into fishless Norwegian mountain lakes 7,000 years ago
stories.summary: In a remarkable feat 7,000 years ago, ancient hunter-gatherers transformed Norwegian mountain lakes by introducing brown trout. Archaeologists discovered remnants of intricate fish traps from 5000 BC, highlighting how these early communities intentionally manipulated their ecosystems for sustainable food resources. They even transported live fish through rugged landscapes, paving the way for the fisheries we know today, where anglers still catch their ancient descendants.

stories.title: Scientists found an anemone that can distinguish helpful bacteria from harmful bacteria without...

stories.title: Global Market: Japan's Nikkei rebounds as bargain buying lifts stocks after sharp weekly selloff
stories.summary: Japan's Nikkei share average advanced on Tuesday, recovering from last week's steep slide. Investors bought beaten-down stocks after a market holiday, boosting the index. Global sentiment remained cautious due to rising oil prices and inflation concerns. Focus now shifts to upcoming second-quarter earnings from major U.S. technology firms. Analysts noted the gains reflected a technical rebound ahead of key corporate results.

stories.title: Cause for concern? HDFC Bank is lagging ICICI Bank where it matters as investors count Rs 5 lakh crore loss
stories.summary: HDFC Bank's stock has declined significantly this year, losing considerable investor value. Analysts note a widening operating gap between HDFC Bank and ICICI Bank. Margins are under pressure for HDFC Bank, while loan growth lags peers. ICICI Bank shows stronger earnings, better margins, and faster credit growth. Investors await margin recovery and management stability for HDFC Bank.

stories.title: UltraTech Cement shares gain 2% after Q1 results. Why Nuvama, other brokerages raised target?
stories.summary: UltraTech Cement shares rose after the company posted a 17% year-on-year rise in Q1 FY27 consolidated net profit to Rs 2,599 crore and a 16% increase in revenue to Rs 24,648 crore. The stock also gained after multiple brokerages raised their target prices, encouraged by strong earnings and management guidance

stories.title: Paytm shares gain 3% after Q1 results. What are Goldman Sachs, Citi and CLSA saying?
stories.summary: Paytm shares rose 2.7% after the fintech firm reported a 79% year-on-year jump in Q1 consolidated net profit to Rs 220 crore. Revenue from operations increased 28% to Rs 2,448 crore, while total income rose 22% to Rs 2,630 crore. Profit before tax climbed to Rs 247 crore, reflecting stronger operating performance both year-on-year and quarter-on-quarter.

stories.title: South Korea’s Kospi jumps 4% after 31% crash from June peak. What’s ahead?
stories.summary: South Korea's Kospi index saw a significant jump, recovering some losses after a sharp decline. Shares of chipmakers like Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix drove this recent market recovery. However, the index remains in a bear market, reflecting investor caution and wild swings. Authorities have intervened to cool speculative fever and curb leveraged trading. Analysts suggest foreign investor selloffs are moderating, with a neutral stance on the market.

stories.title: Sensex falls over 50 points, Nifty below 24,250 despite Iran-US mediation efforts
stories.summary: Indian stock markets saw marginal losses on Tuesday as Sensex and Nifty traded flat. Broader markets, however, opened with modest gains, bucking the overall trend. Mediation efforts between Iran and the US led to a dip in oil prices below $90 per barrel. Analysts suggest crude oil prices and banking sector earnings will guide market direction. Geopolitical developments and regional tensions remain key factors influencing market sentiment.

stories.title: Nvidia among 6 megacaps that Morningstar says are undervalued. See full list
stories.summary: Morningstar sees six megacap stocks as undervalued opportunities. Nvidia's AI GPU systems are expected to drive strong future growth. Microsoft continues its dominance in productivity and public cloud markets. Amazon's valuation is attractive, influencing the consumer cyclical sector. Meta Platforms and AMD also show promising future demand.

stories.title: IndiGo shares in focus on MoU with CFM International for over 1,000 LEAP engines
stories.summary: IndiGo has signed a significant MoU with CFM International for over 1,000 LEAP-1A engines. This agreement marks the largest single order ever placed for LEAP engines. CFM International will also support IndiGo's new engine maintenance facility. The airline's partnership with CFM International continues its growth phase. This deal strengthens IndiGo's expanding fleet and operational reliability.

stories.title: Caliber Mining & Logistics IPO Day 3: GMP hints at 22% listing gain as issue draws robust investor interest
stories.summary: Caliber Mining & Logistics' Rs 450-crore IPO is in its final bidding day. Investor demand remains strong, with the issue subscribed over 23 times. The grey market indicates a potential listing gain of nearly 22%. Proceeds will fund debt repayment and capital expenditure for machinery. The company expects its stock to debut on exchanges on July 24, 2026.

stories.title: Millworks Technologies shares make stellar debut, list at 90% premium on BSE SME
stories.summary: Millworks Technologies shares: The Rs 160.34 crore IPO attracted overwhelming investor interest, closing with an overall subscription of more than 219 times.

stories.title: Paytm calls off its first-ever bonus issue proposal. Here’s what management said
stories.summary: Paytm parent One 97 Communications has shelved its proposed first-ever bonus share issue for now, opting to focus on business growth and improving profitability to create long-term shareholder value. The decision was taken by the board at its July 20 meeting.

stories.title: SBI Funds IPO jackpot turns 13 employees into crorepatis; top ESOP haul hits Rs 121 crore
stories.summary: SBI Funds IPO has transformed thirteen employees into crorepatis through stock ownership. Deputy Managing Director Devinder Pal Singh received the largest payout valued at Rs 121 crore. Chief Investment Officer Srinivasan Rama Iyer also secured a stake worth approximately Rs 105 crore. Several other senior executives gained substantial wealth from the company's successful public offering. This wealth creation highlights the significant payoff from SBI Funds' employee stock ownership program.

stories.title: Alpine Texworld shares to debut today; GMP signals muted listing
stories.summary: Alpine Texworld shares: The stock is expected to debut with only a marginal gain, as the grey market premium stands at about 1%, implying a listing price of around Rs 106 against the upper issue price of Rs 105. The IPO, which was open from July 14 to July 16, was subscribed 1.40 times overall, reflecting moderate investor demand.

stories.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | Power, electricals and cooling could be biggest winners of India’s $25 billion data-centre boom: Atul Suri
stories.summary: India's data-centre expansion will drive significant investment in physical infrastructure. Power equipment and electricals are poised to benefit from this build-out. Cooling infrastructure and electronics manufacturing services also present key opportunities. This shift favors physical builders over traditional technology service providers. Investors should focus on strength and discipline amid this market rotation.

stories.title: Premier Energies among 5 stocks closed above VWAP, signalling bullish shift
stories.summary: On July 20, five stocks in the Nifty500 pack rallied more than 1.5% above their Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP), according to StockEdge's technical scan data. Trading above the VWAP is widely regarded as a bullish technical signal, reflecting strong buying interest and indicating the potential for further upside.

stories.title: Union Bank of India among 7 stocks flashing bullish signals, hinting at a possible uptrend
stories.summary: On July 20, seven stocks in the Nifty500 appeared on the 'White Marubozu' bullish scanner, according to StockEdge's technical scan data. The bullish candlestick pattern reflects strong buying momentum throughout the trading session, signalling sustained positive sentiment and the potential for a continuation of the upward price trend.

stories.title: UTI AMC's V Srivatsa warns against midcap valuation, says risk-reward better in largecaps
stories.summary: The mid cap also is expected to grow in similar lines and trades at 50% premium to large caps. While the longer-term outlook for mid cap is superior to large caps, the valuation premium is at record high and there could be mean reversion especially when the growth is picking up for the large cap. Hence on a risk reward basis, a large cap looks superior.

PNB among 4 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing

The RSI moved above the 50 mark from lower levels, signalling strengthening price action, improving sentiment and potential short-term buying opportunities for traders tracking bullish technical setups.

stories.title: SBI Funds Management gets 2 buy calls before listing. Why Equirus, Emkay see up to 31% upside
stories.summary: SBI Funds Management has received bullish brokerage coverage ahead of its stock market debut, with Equirus Securities and Emkay Global initiating coverage with 'Long' and 'Buy' ratings. Their target prices of Rs 675 and Rs 750 imply up to 31% upside from the IPO price of Rs 574. Brokerages cited the AMC's strong franchise, SBI-backed distribution, sticky SIP flows and robust profitability.

stories.title: Quote of the day by David Beckham: "I have always believed that if you want to achieve anything special in life you have to…" - a powerful lesson on hard work, discipline and why talent alone is never enough
stories.summary: David Beckham's quote highlights continuous hard work as essential for special achievements. He stressed that consistent effort, not just occasional bursts, builds lasting success. Talent provides a head start, but dedication often surpasses natural ability over time. Beckham's career, including overcoming setbacks, exemplifies this philosophy in action. Modern culture often portrays success as instant, but real achievements develop quietly through dedication.

stories.title: Who is Jelicia Westhoff? Maduka Okoye’s baby mama accuses Nigerian goalkeeper of cheating and abuse amid Cardi B rumours
stories.summary: Jelicia Westhoff has found herself in the spotlight after making a series of serious claims against Maduka Okoye. Her posts appeared shortly after the Nigerian goalkeeper was linked to Cardi B following public sightings in Italy. Here's a closer look at who Westhoff is, her relationship with Okoye, and what led to the latest controversy.

stories.title: Quote of the day by Lamine Yamal: "My mother had me at 16, and my father searched for ways to bring food home. That's real pressure. All I have to do is…" - a powerful lesson on perspective, family sacrifice and why football is not the hardest battle his family has faced
stories.summary: Teenage footballer Lamine Yamal shared his perspective on pressure during the World Cup build-up. He contrasted his career with his mother's early parenthood and father's past hardships. Yamal views his demanding football career as a privilege compared to their struggles. This humility places his own challenges within a broader family context. His words offer a powerful reflection on gratitude and perspective beyond the sport.
